title: Anime Figures
tips: Check box quality — official boxes have sharp printing, consistent colors, and proper logos
Look for official stickers from manufacturers (Good Smile Company, Bandai, Kotobukiya, etc.)
Compare with photos on MyFigureCollection (MFC) before purchasing
If the price seems too good to be true, it is probably a bootleg
Check base quality and paint detail — bootlegs have rough seams, uneven paint, and flimsy bases
Official figures typically cost $30+ for prize figures, $100+ for scales

title: Trading Cards
tips: Verify holographic patterns match official cards — fakes often have wrong holo type
Check card thickness and feel — real cards have consistent stock weight
Compare font style and spacing with official card databases
Buy from TCG-specific stores or verified sellers on established platforms
Check set numbers and card codes against official databases
Sealed products should have official shrink wrap and seals

title: Clothing & Fashion
tips: Verify brand tags and stitching quality — licensed goods have proper labels
Check if the design is actually licensed by the IP holder
Reverse image search the design to see if it is stolen fan art
Avoid sites known for printing stolen fan art on cheap blanks
Official anime clothing collabs are announced by the brand or IP holder
Check if the store has a verifiable business registration

title: General Tips
tips: Always check Reddit threads before buying from unknown shops
Use PayPal for buyer protection when possible
Check Trustpilot and review aggregators for seller reputation
Verify the site has SSL (https) and a verifiable company registration
If the price seems too low for the item, it is probably counterfeit
Be wary of sites with aggressive countdown timers and "limited stock" pressure
Search the shop name + "scam" or "fake" before ordering
For high-value items, request photos of the actual item and authentication marks
